nuggets.headline: Before the helmets, Daft Punk was a lo-fi indie punk band called Darlin'
nuggets.text: Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo met at secondary school in Paris in 1987, bonding over music and film. At 17, they formed Darlin', a lo-fi indie punk band named after a Beach Boys song. After their single 'Cindy So Loud' received a negative review in *Melody Maker*, which described it as 'daft punk,' the duo loved the phrase and adopted it for their next musical venture, embracing the thriving Parisian rave scene.

source.title: The Untold Truth Of Daft Punk
source.quoteSnippet: In 1993, Homem-Christo and Bangalter adopted "Daft Punk" as the name for their next musical venture.

nuggets.imageCaption: Daft Punk's iconic robot helmets provide acclaim and anonymity.

nuggets.headline: 'Around the World' repeats its title 144 times, engineered for pure groove
nuggets.text: You're hearing 'Around the World,' a global phenomenon from their debut album *Homework* that dominated dance charts upon its 1997 release. Daft Punk described its creation as 'making a Chic record with a talk box and just playing the bass on the synthesizer'. The track's hypnotic groove, driven by a steady bassline and that iconic robotic vocal, repeats 'around the world' a staggering 144 times on the album version, a masterclass in infectious rhythm.

source.title: Daft Punk ‘Around the World’: A Timeless Electronic Anthem
source.quoteSnippet: The album version echoes the phrase 'around the world' a staggering 144 times.

nuggets.imageCaption: Michel Gondry's video for 'Around the World' features synchronized dancers.

nuggets.headline: If you love this groove, check out Stardust's timeless French house classic
nuggets.text: If 'Around the World' has you moving, you'll absolutely love Stardust. This French house supergroup featured Daft Punk's own Thomas Bangalter, and their hit 'Music Sounds Better with You' shares that irresistible, repetitive vocal hook and funky, dancefloor-ready production that makes Daft Punk so catchy. It's pure, accessible French house with a similar warmth and undeniable rhythm that builds and evolves, perfect for continuing that groove.

source.title: The enduring legacy of Stardust’s ‘Music Sounds Better With You’
source.quoteSnippet: Stardust, the short-lived French house supergroup consisting of Thomas Bangalter, Alan Braxe and Benjamin Diamond...

nuggets.imageCaption: Stardust, featuring Thomas Bangalter, created a French house classic.

artistSummary: Daft Punk, the iconic French electronic music duo of Thomas Bangalter and Guy-Manuel de Homem-Christo, rose from a punk band to global EDM titans [CIT 0]. Renowned for their universally respected records and maintaining anonymity through robot helmets, they consistently delivered genre-defying music while shifting culture [CIT 0, CIT 1].
